author: iuga
description: Turn your notes into a conversation. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Esteban Del Boca
manifest.description: Turn your notes into a conversation.
manifest.name: Porygon

name: Porygon

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- Fixed the chat getting stuck when you switched sessions mid-reply. Using `/resume` or `/new` while the assistant was still responding could leave the loading indicator spinning forever, block new messages, and even save the abandoned reply into the wrong session. Switching now cleanly stops the in-flight response.
- Fixed the resume screen showing an empty, broken-looking list on startup. The session picker now opens instantly with a loading bar and fills in your sessions from newest to oldest as they load.

### Performance Improvements
- Opening `/resume` is now noticeably smoother in large vaults. The session list fills in without the janky, repeated redraws that previously slowed it down.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
The TypeScript toolchain upgrade (typescript-eslint, then TypeScript 6.0) surfaced 49 type-aware lint errors and broke the build. The root cause was configuration, not code: the compiler targeted an outdated standard-library level, so modern JS built-ins were treated as untyped.
So...
Upgrade the lint/type toolchain and fix the configuration it exposed, so lint and build pass cleanly again. Also resolves three pre-existing type errors the old build was silently skipping.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- Fixed the `/resume` command not working on some vaults. The session picker would silently fail to open with nothing in the console to explain why; it now logs a clear error so the problematic saved session can be identified.
- Fixed all command popups (slash commands, file mentions, session resume, prompts, and notifications) failing silently when they couldn't open. Failures are now logged to the console, and a popup that fails to open no longer blocks the next one from appearing.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What's Changed
- **Smarter memory**: The assistant now recognizes durable preferences on its own and saves them the moment you mention them standing instructions like "always flag critical info" stick around instead of being forgotten after the session.
- **Focused note suggestions**: Responses now highlight a single key note to open at the end, instead of stacking multiple file widgets. When several notes are relevant, they appear as inline links you can click.

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed wikilinks inside callout widgets showing as plain text. Links like `[[Some Note]]` are now clickable and resolve correctly.
- Fixed the assistant occasionally emitting unsupported widget types that silently rendered nothing. It now falls back to readable prose or a wikilink instead of leaving blank output.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What's Changed
- **Unified note search**: Keyword and semantic search are now a single search tool, giving the assistant one consistent way to find your notes. Results combine exact keyword matches (BM25) and meaning-based matching at once, so you get faster and more relevant answers. Search activates once you've configured an embeddings model and run indexing.
- Note retrieval now uses a hybrid search index instead of scanning every note in memory, making searches noticeably faster and more relevant on larger vaults.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What's Changed
- **Inline file links in replies**: When the assistant references, creates, or updates a note, it can now drop a rich file card right in the chat. The card shows the note's name and a short preview of its content, so you can jump straight to the source in one click instead of hunting for a plain link.
- **Callout highlights**: The assistant can now spotlight an important passage as a colored callout with a matching icon, picked to fit the message, an idea, a key insight, a note, a success, a hot topic, a warning, a danger, or a quotation. When the highlight comes from one of your notes, the whole callout becomes clickable and opens that note. Quotations preserve the original wording.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- Fixed note citations that appeared as plain, unclickable text. In some answers the assistant referenced a note but the link rendered as raw text, so you couldn't open the cited note. Citations now render as clickable links that take you straight to the note.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- Fixed note citations rendering as plain, unclickable text. When the assistant referenced a vault note whose name contained spaces (e.g. "Weekly Team Notes"), the link was broken and could not be opened. Citations now render as clickable links that take you straight to the note. External links are left untouched.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What's Changed
- **Configurable Porygon folder**: You can now choose where Porygon keeps its internal notes (chat sessions and skills) instead of being locked to the `porygon/` folder. Set a custom path under **Settings → Porygon → Advanced** to match your vault's organization. Existing vaults keep working unchanged — the default stays `porygon`.
  - Applying a new path **moves your existing folder and all its contents** to the new location, and internal links are updated automatically.
  - Your current folder is protected: Porygon won't overwrite a note or a folder that already has files in it, so you can't accidentally clobber existing content.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- **Fixed a cross-vault data leak in semantic search.** The RAG index was stored in a single database shared by every vault in the same Obsidian install, so opening one vault could expose another vault's indexed note content and embeddings. Each vault now gets its own isolated, schema-versioned index database, closing the leak. Any previously shared index is deleted automatically on first launch.
- **Internal Porygon notes are no longer indexed or surfaced in search.** Porygon's own managed files under `porygon/` (sessions, skills) were being embedded and could appear in semantic search results. They are now excluded unconditionally, and any already-indexed internal notes are purged automatically.

### Notes ( Breaking Changes )
- After upgrading, each vault rebuilds its index once on next launch. Semantic search may be briefly incomplete while re-indexing finishes — no action needed.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What's Changed
- **Markdown in your messages**: Your own chat messages now render as full markdown — headings, lists, tables, links, and code blocks all display formatted instead of raw text. This applies both when you send a message and when you resume a saved session, so past conversations look right too.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- Fixed chat rendering in pop-out windows. Messages and Markdown content now display correctly when the assistant is opened in a separate Obsidian window.

### Performance Improvements
- Reduced style-recalculation overhead when hovering or focusing message actions, for smoother interaction in long conversations.

### Breaking Changes
- **Minimum Obsidian version**: This release now requires Obsidian 1.8.7 or newer. Update Obsidian before upgrading.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/iuga/obsidian-porygon/compare/0.13.0...0.13.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What's Changed
- **Copy agent responses**: Finished answers now show a hover-revealed actions bar with a one-click copy-to-clipboard button, so you can grab a reply without selecting text by hand.
- **Automatic conversation saving**: Conversations now persist automatically after every completed, cancelled, or failed turn, closing Obsidian no longer loses your work. The manual `/save` command has been removed, and switching with `/new` or resuming is now silent.

### Breaking Changes
- **`/save` removed and `/sessions` renamed to `/resume`**: Saving is now automatic, so `/save` no longer exists. Use `/resume` to load and open a previous conversation.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/iuga/obsidian-porygon/compare/0.12.0...0.13.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What's Changed
- **Stop a streaming reply**: You can now cancel an in-flight response while the assistant is replying. The send button turns into a stop button during streaming. Cancel while it's still connecting and the placeholder disappears; cancel mid-reply and the partial text is kept so you can continue the conversation from there.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/iuga/obsidian-porygon/compare/0.11.0...0.12.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Version 0.11.0 brings a more reliable and consistent in-app experience. Saving sessions is now hardened: the new feedback popover surfaces save results (error, info, or hint) so `/save` no longer fails silently, falling back to an empty title when generation fails and retrying on a stale-cache create race. Under the hood, all five popovers (mention, slash, session, ask, and feedback) are now unified behind a shared `PopoverHost` that owns a single open popover and enforces mutual exclusion automatically, eliminating the stacking-popover bug class and manual cross-close bookkeeping. Finally, the assistant's `ask_user` prompts now surface decision criteria and priorities rather than pre-baked outcomes, so choices better reflect your own preferences.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/iuga/obsidian-porygon/compare/0.10.0...0.11.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Features
*   **UI/UX**: Updated the composer input placeholder message in `src/view.ts` to improve clarity and user guidance: `"How can I help you today? • / for commands • @ for mentions"`.
*   `feat(settings):` Added support for "graded thinking effort with capability gating" in settings.

### Fixes
*   **`feat(composer):`** Fixed the mention popover to close correctly when clicking outside the component.
*   **`style(popover):`** Applied an accent border style on filter input when it gains focus.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Refactors
- **Split agent module and decoupled from Ollama** via a provider registry, enabling support for additional model providers.

### Fixes
- **Composer:** Close the mention popover when clicking outside (composer loses focus).
- **Composer:** Show the accent shadow only on focus.

### Chores
- Added pre-commit hooks to run lint and build before each commit, preventing CI/CD failures.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
When the user sends a message, the chat must smooth-scroll so the new user message sits at the very top of the chat container, with mentions + user message + thinking + tools + answer visible below it; the thinking block is just an inline (non-scrolling) collapsible component that expands freely as part of the message; if the streamed reply grows beyond the viewport the scroll naturally bottom-follows the new content with no empty space below; any explicit user scroll input cancels the auto-scroll and hands control fully back to the user.

The chat view must keep the UI responsive while the agent streams a reply, even on long threads: streamed content and thinking tokens have to render into the active bubble as markdown in real time, without ever re-rendering past messages, with live markdown updates coalesced per animation frame and serialized so the async `MarkdownRenderer.render` never races on the same target. 

The "Thinking..." placeholder must appear immediately when the user sends, without waiting on the Ollama health check, and historical thinking bubbles must not pay markdown-render cost while collapsed. 

The LangChain agent must be built once for the plugin lifetime (with per-turn system prompt injected dynamically) and rebuilt only when the user changes the Ollama host, chat model, or thinking setting, while preserving existing per-conversation checkpointing.

When the thinking block was taller than the viewport, collapsing it on first answer delta shrank the message and left the streaming answer above the viewport. Re-pin the user message to the top so the answer fills the freed space below.

Pinned `uuid` to `^11.1.1` via npm `overrides` to remediate GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq (silent out-of-bounds writes in `v3`/`v5`/`v6`), since `@langchain/langgraph`, `langgraph-checkpoint`, and `langgraph-sdk` still pull vulnerable `uuid` versions transitively and no patched upstream release is available yet.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Each onboarding step now shows a muted inline hint under the question (default URL for the host step, `ollama run` commands for the chat and embedding steps), and going back to a completed step re-shows its question instead of the saved value so the guidance stays visible. A new fourth step, **Experience**, exposes a single dropdown that derives `ollamaThinking`, `showToolUsage`, and `yolo` from a preset — Minimal, Balanced, Verbose (default), or YOLO — persisted in a new `experience` setting, and the default embedding model is now `qwen3-embedding`. Two bugs are also fixed: `RagIndexer` and `RagSemanticSearchService` compared settings against the same live reference and never detected post-onboarding changes, so the index never ran (they now snapshot the watched fields and short-circuit when no embedding model is set); and `save_memory` rejected `id: null` because its schema only allowed `string | undefined`, which now accepts `null` and treats it as "append a new memory."

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Porygon now remembers things about you across sessions through a file-backed long-term memory stored in plugin settings. Entries live in a user-editable **Memories** textarea wrapped in `<memories>` tags, sorted by importance (high, medium, low) and recency, and the block is injected into the system prompt every turn (capped at the top 50 entries, with a note when older entries are skipped). A `save_memory` agent tool appends, replaces, or deletes entries by id; saves are silent. Timestamps are stored in UTC and the system prompt also exposes a `<context>` block with local datetime, UTC datetime, IANA timezone, and operating system. Memories are sanitized on every save and load so malformed lines never reach disk, and the textarea persists through Obsidian's debouncer to coalesce keystrokes. The chat and embedding model fields in settings are now dropdowns populated from the live Ollama model list, refreshed when the host changes and matching the onboarding UX. The legacy `current_timestamp` tool is removed since the same information now lives in the context block.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## QoL improvements
Porygon's chat now communicates its state more honestly. While the agent is working, a subtle loading indicator at the top of the panel makes it clear whether it's your turn or Porygon's. The placeholder bubble shown right after you hit send is renamed from `Thinking...` to `Connecting...`, since the agent hasn't actually started thinking yet. The tool-usage panel is leaner too: each entry now shows just the intent, dropping the redundant tool name so the list reads like a clean activity log.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Porygon can now pause mid-task and ask you a focused question when it genuinely needs your input, instead of guessing or producing a half-relevant answer. A small popover appears in the chat composer with the question and 2–4 short options; you can click an option, pick one with number keys or arrow keys plus Enter, type a free-form reply, or press Escape to let Porygon continue on its own. Once you answer, Porygon resumes exactly where it left off.

The same pause-and-ask flow also gates destructive vault actions: creating a folder, creating or editing a note, and renaming or moving files now prompt for **Approve** or **Deny**, and you can type a short free-form reason to deny with feedback so Porygon adjusts its next step. A new **YOLO mode** setting auto-approves these prompts when you want Porygon to move without interruptions, and the setting is read live so you can toggle it mid-conversation.

The `list` tool now returns folders alongside notes, so Porygon can find and act on folders by name. The selected option in the prompt uses your theme's accent color for clear contrast on both light and dark themes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Porygon now uses a LangGraph `MemorySaver` checkpointer keyed by a per-session UUID so the agent remembers prior turns within a session without resending full history each call. Saved sessions store their metadata as Obsidian-native YAML frontmatter (`id`, `title`, and a flat `mentions:` list of vault paths) instead of a custom comment block, making it queryable through the Properties panel, Dataview, and Bases; on load, mentioned notes and folders are auto-resolved from their paths and re-injected as file context. **Breaking change:** session files saved before 0.5.0 (custom `%%porygon:metadata%%` blocks and timestamp-based filenames) are no longer readable — start fresh or migrate manually.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Porygon introduces a skills system that lets the agent extend its capabilities through Markdown files with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`) stored under `porygon/skills/` in the vault. Bundled skills shipped with the plugin are copied into the vault on first launch, parsed once at startup via a new `SkillsService` (after `onLayoutReady` so the vault is fully scanned) and cached, refreshing only when vault create/modify/delete/rename events touch the skills folder. The list is injected into the system prompt as an `<available_skills>` block, and a new `load_skill` agent tool reads the full skill body (without frontmatter) on demand using the exact location advertised in that block.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Porygon adds four vault-aware agent tools: `create_folder` for creating folders with Obsidian `Vault.createFolder`, `copy` for duplicating files or folders with `Vault.copy`, `active_file` for resolving the currently focused Obsidian file when users refer to “this note,” and `backlinks` for listing notes that link to a target note from Obsidian `MetadataCache.resolvedLinks`. These tools extend the assistant from note search/editing into vault organization and graph-aware navigation while preserving exact path validation, no filename guessing, and JSON tool results for transparent agent behavior.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Porygon fixes a startup double-counting bug in the semantic index, prevents re-embedding of unchanged notes on launch and on stray re-enqueues, and makes the settings status row always reflect the real number of indexed and total notes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.3.0: Local semantic search over the vault
Porygon adds a local RAG foundation for semantic vault search using the same Ollama provider as chat: Markdown notes are chunked with LangChain text splitters, embedded with the configured Ollama embeddings model, and persisted in an IndexedDB-backed index under `src/rag/*`. The index is maintained in the background through startup reconciliation and Obsidian vault create/modify/delete/rename events, supports ignored-path settings and model/host freshness checks, exposes index status in settings, and adds a `semantic_search` agent tool that returns matching note paths, wikilinks, scores, and snippets for contextual note discovery while keeping existing exact `list`, `search`, and `view` tools as fallbacks.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Polishing the linting and release process to be approved by the Obsidian team

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: `0.2.6` improved responsiveness by immediately showing a `Thinking...` Porygon answer bubble until the first streamed token, reasoning delta, or tool intent arrives; added a centered deterministic daily empty-chat quote and a clearer composer placeholder for `/` commands and `@` mentions; introduced a safe `rename(intent, source_path, destination_path)` agent tool that uses Obsidian `FileManager.renameFile` and documents its privacy impact; and addressed Obsidian community plugin publishing feedback by removing interval-based Ollama polling, using event-based health checks, replacing newer APIs that exceeded `minAppVersion`, fixing UI construction warnings, and adding an attested GitHub Actions release workflow for `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.

releases.author: iuga
releases.description: Linting the code to be approved by Obsidian

releases.author: iuga
releases.description: This release improves Porygon’s context and command workflow with a redesigned mention popup that supports filtering, keyboard navigation, icons, active-note mentions, folder mentions, and direct child Markdown file attachment for folder context. It also adds slash commands with a searchable popup, including `/new` for starting a new conversation with an optional save prompt and `/save` for writing the current visible conversation to `porygon/conversation_{timestamp}.md`. Bug fixes include using the first message timestamp instead of message text for saved conversation filenames, overwriting saved conversations on repeated saves to match the current UI state, and improving mention chip layout so chips render horizontally, wrap cleanly, and use smaller text.

releases.author: iuga
releases.description:
- Marks Porygon as desktop-only to better reflect current dependency support.
- Improves release guideline alignment by removing production console logging from the edit tool.
- Updates warning and onboarding hint styles to use Obsidian theme variables instead of hardcoded colors.
- Makes thinking and tool-usage sections use Obsidian’s small UI font size while keeping chat message text unchanged.
- Fixes the view close lifecycle method to satisfy TypeScript without an unnecessary `async`.
- Polishes the plugin description and bumps release metadata to `0.2.3`.

releases.author: iuga
releases.description: Manifest and package.json version management to add the plugin in the registry

releases.author: iuga
releases.description: Porygon is a privacy-first Obsidian chat plugin powered by local Ollama models, with guided setup, streamed Markdown responses, selectable chat content, optional thinking output, and explicit note mentions for grounded context. This release adds a LangGraph/LangChain agent architecture, vault-aware tools for listing, searching, viewing, and safely editing notes, transparent tool usage reporting, split system/personal prompts stored as readable Markdown, a larger personal prompt editor, improved stability, lint-clean builds, and a complete product rename from The Obsidian Assistant to Porygon.
